title: Atom Editor Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and shortcuts for Atom Editor users.

commands.description: Install an Atom package via command line.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to add new functionality to Atom by installing a package.

commands.description: Uninstall an Atom package via command line.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to remove a package that is no longer needed.
Use to review which packages are currently installed in your Atom editor.

commands.description: Start Atom in safe mode, disabling all packages.
commands.scenario: Use when troubleshooting issues in Atom to determine if a package is causing problems.

commands.description: Open the current directory in Atom.
commands.scenario: Use to quickly open a project folder in Atom from the terminal.

commands.description: Toggle the tree view in Atom.
commands.scenario: Use to show or hide the file tree view in Atom for better focus or navigation.

commands.description: Open the command palette in Atom.
commands.scenario: Use to access all available commands in Atom quickly.

commands.description: Open the settings view in Atom.
commands.scenario: Use to adjust settings and preferences in Atom.

commands.description: Open the developer tools in Atom.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to debug Atom itself or a package.
Use to ensure all your Atom packages are up to date with the latest versions.

commands.description: Upgrade Atom to the latest version.
commands.scenario: Use to get the latest features and fixes in Atom.

commands.description: Toggle the markdown preview in Atom.
commands.scenario: Use to preview markdown files directly within Atom.
